part_encoding.notes: US Extreme Battle (ULUS10034) ~350 parts; JP International (ULJS19001) 427 parts. Part IDs are shared numbering across regions. Frame parts (Core/Head/Arms/Legs/FCS/Generator/Booster/Radiator) max out below 300 in both versions; IDs 350-427 are exclusively weapons and option parts.

notes.positional_scan: User upload path uses positional scan (no FORMULA F required). Offsets below are relative to the 12-byte null-terminated AC name anchor byte.
notes.all_offsets_confirmed: All offsets confirmed via targeted PPSSPP .ppst save diffs at 0x102402c cluster (assembly/working-copy). Head(+76)/Core(+78) confirmed via coreandradonly.ppst. Arms(+80)/Legs_Struct(+82) confirmed via gaea_baseline frame-only save. Booster(+84)/FCS(+86)/Generator(+88)/Radiator(+90) confirmed via gaea_autoequip auto-fill diff. All weapon slots confirmed via single-weapon targeted saves. Default AC1 (defaultac1.ppst) cross-validated all values.
notes.legs_dual_storage: Legs ID appears at both -16 (offLegs, used for validation/extraction) and +82 (Legs_Struct, in-struct copy filled alongside Head/Core/Arms at frame assembly time).
notes.ac_struct_stride: 0x180 (384 bytes) between consecutive ACs in a team. 5 ACs per team.
notes.hangar_slots: Arm_R_Hangar(+104) and Arm_L_Hangar(+106) always 0 in all known saves.

color_groups.notes: 4 color groups × 2 regions. Each group: 6 slots × 16 bytes. Color index = uint32 LE at slot+0 (NOT +8 — prior docs were wrong). Slot 0=AC1…slot 5=AC6. AC stride within group: 0x10. US-to-JP offset is +0x690 for all 4 groups (same delta as stage cursor). GENERAL in SAMPLE COLOR sets both frame_main AND frame_secondary simultaneously. Per-part frame breakdown (HEAD/CORE/etc.) is cosmetic UI only — no separate RAM groups exist for individual frame parts.

weapon_color_indices.note: US and JP indices differ for GREEN and above. JP has one extra colour before GREEN shifting the table. US indices: 0=BLACK 1=RED 2=YELLOW 3=GREEN 4=BLUE 5=WHITE. JP indices: 0=BLACK 1=RED 2=YELLOW 3=? 4=GREEN(confirmed 2026-04-30 via AC2 WPN-MAIN diff slot4) 5=BLUE(tentative) 6=WHITE(tentative).

JP.4: GREEN (confirmed 2026-04-30: AC2 WPN-MAIN=4 rendered green in AC TEST)

frame_color_indices.note_JP: JP: 0=default(blue/yellow), 2=RED (confirmed via SAMPLE COLOR GENERAL save). JP has more unlocked frame colors so RED shifts to index 2.

weapon_main.confirmed: US via wc_red2 vs wc_yellow2 diff; JP via jp_wpnred vs jp_wpnyellow diff

weapon_secondary.confirmed: US via wc_red2 vs wc_yellow2 diff; JP via jp_wpnred vs jp_wpnyellow diff

frame_main.confirmed: US via s2_color1 vs s2 diff (idx changed 0→1); JP via jp_frmred vs jp_wpnred diff (idx changed 0→2)

frame_secondary.confirmed: US via s2_color1 vs s2 diff (FRM-SEC did NOT change → US default idx=0 is shared); JP via jp_frmred vs jp_wpnred diff (idx changed 0→2)

T2_groups.notes: T2 weapon color is stored in TWO separate save-persistent memory blocks — both must be written. Block-A 0x08EC8050, Block-C 0x090F13D0; per-AC stride 0x180, inner slot stride 0x10, 6 active slots per AC. CONFIRMED 2026-05-01 via ULJS19001_1.00_0 (slot0 all-white 5-AC roster) ppst diff. NOTE: 0x0885F628 is a battle-runtime render-table address (battle-only, not save-persistent) — it was incorrectly recorded as the primary address and has been corrected in code. NOTE: 0x08869F98 was INVALID (struct pointers). The UI preset table (0x08869C50) shows garbage in AC TEST saves.

weapon_main.status: JP CONFIRMED 2026-05-01 via ppst diff (all-white roster); US unknown

custom_rgb_struct.notes: Separate from the SAMPLE COLOR preset index arrays. Stores per-part custom RGB values for a loaded AC. FULLY CONFIRMED 2026-05-01 via ULJS19001_1.00_7 (red team: R=50/100/150/175/200 per sub-field, G=B=0) and ULJS19001_1.00_8 (green team: G=50/100/150/175/200, R=B=0). R/G/B byte positions within each 3-byte sub-field triplet confirmed unambiguously.

struct_layout.block_size: 0x50 bytes per loaded AC (5 rows x 0x10)
struct_layout.ac_stride: 0x180 per AC (both T1 and T2 use same per-AC stride)
struct_layout.row_format: BASE_R(1B) BASE_G(1B) BASE_B(1B) AID_R(1B) AID_G(1B) AID_B(1B) OPT_R(1B) OPT_G(1B) OPT_B(1B) DET_R(1B) DET_G(1B) DET_B(1B) JOINT_R(1B) JOINT_G(1B) JOINT_B(1B) pad(1B) [CONFIRMED: R=byte[i*3], G=byte[i*3+1], B=byte[i*3+2] for sub-field i]

T1_block_JP.note: T1 (ALPHA/GRID1/player team) custom RGB static buffer. CONFIRMED 2026-05-01 via ULJS19001_1.00_7.ppst scan: 5 consecutive full-match blocks at 0x08824650, 0x088247D0, 0x08824950, 0x08824AD0, 0x08824C50 (stride 0x180). Prior sessions incorrectly recorded 0x08824050 (= T2 AC1 base). T1_AC1 base = 0x08824650.
T1_block_JP.confirmed: ULJS19001_1.00_7.ppst scan (re_dump_region.py): 5 FULL MATCH blocks at indices +4 through +8 from 0x08824050 = T1 AC1-5

T2_block_JP.note: T2 (OMEGA/GRID2/opponent team) custom RGB in-battle buffer. T2 AC1-5 occupy slots 0-4 at stride 0x180 from 0x08824050. Copy B at 0x0885FF94 is a second live copy. OVERLAP: T2_AC5 base = 0x08824050 + 4*0x180 = 0x08824650 = T1_AC1 base. In the same PPSSPP instance, injecting T1_AC1 and T2_AC5 writes to the same physical address.

copy_A.confirmed: 2026-05-01 scan: T2 AC3/AC4 slots at +2/+3 from base show pre-existing test color data

T2_block_JP.inject_timing: write to BOTH copies during in-battle state

OVERLAP.note: T1_AC1 and T2_copy_A_AC5 share physical address 0x08824650. Within a single PPSSPP instance, injecting colors for both T1_AC1 (index 0) and T2_AC5 (index 4) writes to the same 0x50-byte region. Forbidden pairing: T1_ACIndex=0 vs T2_ACIndex=4.

arena_unlock.notes: Two identical uint32[18] lists (null-terminated) in RAM. Starter arenas 1-7 always available (not in list). Unlockable arenas 8-21 written as IDs in unlock order. US-to-JP offset: +0x690. Confirmed via JP fully-unlocked ppst vs US locked ppst diff.

won_battles.notes: Sorted null-terminated list of uint64 entries {arena_id: uint32 LE, pad_zero: uint32 LE} written by the game when T1 (home team) wins a league (ranking) battle. US confirmed: AC_Garage.bin, 23 entries IDs 1-26 minus {17,18,21}. JP confirmed: ppst diff ULJS19001_1.00_2 (fresh pre-win) vs ULJS19001_1.00_4 (post-win) — 23 entries appeared at 0x088688BC matching US data; count at 0x088690B8 changed to 23. US-to-JP offset = +0x960 (NOT +0x690 — previous assumption 0x088685EC was a false positive; that VA contained unrelated garbage values that coincidentally looked like small integers). List-to-count delta +0x7FC is the same for both regions.
won_battles.confirmed_via: AC_Garage.bin raw RAM dump (25MB) offset=VA-0x08000000; ppst ULJS19001_1.00_0.ppst decompressed

stage_cursor.notes: uint32 LE stage index (0-based). Stage table base: 0x08B261D4 (20 stages).
